articles.title: Peel police investigating shooting in Brampton
articles.summary: Peel police are investigating after a shooting in Brampton. Officers were called to Dixie Road and Lisa Street just after 9 p.m. for reports of shooting. There’s been no information released about whether someone was injured in the shooting. No suspect information has been released, but there is no known threat to public safety. Police […]

articles.title: Committee grants variance to developer despite ongoing investigation at Toronto property
articles.summary: Residents of a York neighbourhood are speaking out after Toronto’s Committee of Adjustment approved variances for a construction company that’s been accused of cutting down trees without permission. There are currently nine investigations open into developer Modcity’s conduct, including one in which they were allegedly caught by neighbours cutting down a tree at a property they d

articles.title: Number of charges reduced for some accused in Project South police corruption probe
articles.summary: The number of charges against some of the accused in a sweeping organized crime and police corruption probe in the GTA has been reduced as the case against them moves forward in Superior Court. Seven Toronto police officers and one retired constable were among 27 suspects charged under Project South, a massive investigation by York […]

articles.title: Newly retired Brampton couple wins $70M Lotto Max jackpot
articles.summary: A newly retired Brampton couple says retirement will be "next level" now they have won a life-changing $70-million Lotto Max jackpot.

articles.title: Toronto man arrested for allegedly robbing North York store with concealed ‘blunt object’
articles.summary: Toronto police have arrested a man in connection with a retail robbery investigation in North York. Police were called to the area of Islington Avenue and Millwick Drive at around 11:45 a.m. on July 14 for a robbery at a store. A man allegedly entered a store wearing a mask to hide his identity. He approached […]

articles.title: Toronto man charged after allegedly robbing store with rock: police
articles.summary: Toronto police officers say they were called to a business in the Fort York Boulevard and Bathurst Street area late on July 1.

articles.title: $24K worth of cocaine seized, 6 people charged in GTA drug and firearm trafficking investigation
articles.summary: Six people have been charged in connection with an investigation into cocaine and firearm trafficking in York Region and the Greater Toronto Area. York Regional Police began investigating in 2025 after officers became aware of a person involved in trafficking controlled substances and firearms in York Region and surrounding areas. Investigators identified more suspects over […]

articles.title: Receipt left inside allegedly stolen SUV leads Hamilton police to 16‑year‑old suspect
articles.summary: Hamilton police say a seemingly ordinary fast‑food receipt left inside a stolen vehicle helped officers track down and arrest a 16‑year‑old boy in a theft investigation earlier this week. Officers were called around 7:50 a.m. on Monday, July 21, to the area of Hwy. 8 and King Street East after reports of an abandoned black […]

articles.title: Fire at Scarborough commercial plaza being investigated as suspicious, authorities say
articles.summary: What started as a vehicle fire spread to a commercial plaza near Eglinton Avenue E. and Kennedy Road early Wednesday morning, Toronto Fire says.

articles.title: How wildfire smoke travels from Ontario to New York City — and turns distant city skies orange
articles.summary: Wildfires that have torched huge areas of forest and devastated communities are also sending smoke far and wide, prompting air quality warnings in distant cities and heated political rhetoric from the U.S. Experts say there's nothing particularly unusual about this summer's smoke patterns — but they say wildfires are getting worse, and that's not likely to stop on either side of the border.

articles.title: Woman, 18, charged with attempted murder after teen stabbed on bus: Peel police
articles.summary: An 18-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder after a teen was stabbed on board a transit bus in Mississauga, Ont, police say.
